last_verdict.note: Logic suite green on the i9-13980HX (Windows .venv python), ~60s. 2026-06-08c: SMART-ROUTING PHASE-2 HARDENING (from a 6-dimension fan-out audit, 19/28 findings confirmed). Landed 3 verified fixes: (1) PII fail-safe -- lowercased ASR 'what is john salary' defeated the [A-Z] name+money rule -> case-insensitive comp-word -> PRIVATE; (2) host-aware cost_order -- host_rank outermost so CN sorts after US; (3) capability_router ON for desktop_gpu_4090 (the shipped device had it off). +3 tests. FULL HANDOFF: docs/session_2026-06-08_smart_routing_phase2.md. ===> ALSO STILL OPEN from phase-1 (owner improvements #2/#3): PROSODY ENDPOINTING is ACTIVATED on the Windows config.local.json (endpoint_detector=prosody) -- needs the owner's LIVE tuning (talk with mid-thought pauses; raise endpoint_incomplete_threshold toward 0.6 if it still replies on a pause). SenseVoice agreement-guard (#3) still open (needs real recorded clips). === PRIOR: DONE 2026-06-08b (STREAMING-TTS LATENCY -- owner improvement #1; merged fix/llm-think-off-tts-latency -> main @b2cddc4, 1395 green). FULL HANDOFF: docs/session_2026-06-08_streaming_tts_thinking_latency.md (read first). HEADLINE: the streaming chain was NEVER the problem -- per-sentence emit -> TTS_REQUEST bus -> engine.speak -> playback is fully incremental and works. The real cause of 'a long story feels like it waits for the whole answer' is that the MAIN tier (gemma4:12b) is a REASONING model: it streams a silent chain-of-thought into a separate `thinking` field BEFORE any `content` token, and OllamaLLM.stream only yields content -> ~9s of dead air for a STORY (which needs no reasoning). MEASURED on gemma4:12b (warmed, 100% GPU): content first-token 9.1s, first spoken sentence 9.5s, full answer 11.2s. FIX: threaded a `think` param through core/llm.py OllamaLLM (top-level chat arg, sent only when set -> non-reasoning models / older Ollama unaffected) + default it OFF in core/llm_factory.build_llms (llm.think=false in config.json, overridable). AFTER (production factory path): first-token 1.8s, first sentence 2.4s, full 6.1s -- a 7s cut; streaming TTS now genuinely helps (audio at 2.4s vs full 6.1s). NOTE: gemma3:4b (fast tier) is NOT a reasoning model (think=false is a graceful no-op). ===> #1 NEXT (owner improvement #2 -- ACTIVATED on the WINDOWS box 2026-06-08b, NEEDS THE OWNER'S LIVE TUNING): PROSODY ENDPOINTING (Smart Turn v3). Owner clarification: this does NOT replace SenseVoice STT -- it's a separate WHEN-is-the-turn-done layer (decides turn boundaries, transcribes nothing), so it CANNOT lower transcription accuracy; if anything it helps by not truncating mid-thought. NOW ON in config.local.json (gitignored, per-machine): endpoint_detector=prosody + endpoint_prosody_model=<abs path to smart-turn-v3.1-cpu.onnx> + endpoint_incomplete_threshold raised 0.3->0.5 so mid-thought pauses (low score) trigger the bounded HOLD; verified the runtime builds ProsodyTurnCompletionDetector (needs_audio) from the merged config; the detector loaded+ran a real human clip -> 0.9134 (complete), thin audio -> 0.5. LIVE-TUNE: run `python -m core --engine sherpa`, talk with mid-thought pauses; if it still replies on a pause raise endpoint_incomplete_threshold toward 0.6; if it holds too long lower it; to cut post-speech latency lower endpoint_min_silence_sec (1.1 -> ~0.5) once happy. Validation knobs from 2026-06-01: complete turns 0.74-0.98, incomplete 0.01-0.56. The model is HUMAN-AUDIO only (flat ~0.97 on TTS) -> synthetic harness can't validate; needs real speech. On OTHER machines it's still OFF (flip the same keys in that machine's config.local.json). The model is HUMAN-AUDIO ONLY (flat ~0.97 on TTS) so the synthetic harness CANNOT validate it -- needs real speech. (B) SenseVoice agreement-guard for STT (owner improvement #3): currently DISABLED (asr_final_backend='') because the 2nd pass hallucinated SHORT clips ('SO'->'I then.'). Proper fix = new core/asr_text.py token-agreement helper gating _final_transcribe (core/engines/sherpa.py:1098). SUBTLETY found this session: a naive overlap guard would WRONGLY reject the legit garbled-correction case the 2nd pass exists for ('Ario der'->'are you there', low overlap) while the hallucinations are the SHORT-clip cases -- the discriminator must key on length/short-clip risk, NOT pure overlap. Needs real recorded STT clips (hallucinations + good corrections) to tune -> perceptual, validate live. (C) Re-enable input_gate.enabled=true for always-on use (config.local.json, currently false for direct-reply testing). (D) housekeeping (carried): core/runlog.py keeps 20 bundles but ~50 committed -> every run shows logs/runs deletions in git (bump SPEAKER_KEEP_RUNS or trim committed bundles). === PRIOR: DONE 2026-06-08 (DEVICE-ADAPTIVE BARGE-IN WORKS on the open laptop speaker; merged fix/barge-raw-mic-and-latency -> main, 1369 green). FULL HANDOFF: docs/session_2026-06-08_device_adaptive_barge_in.md (read first). Owner confirmed 'barge feels good now' -- NORMAL-volume talk-over interrupts reliably on the bare ALC285 mic+speaker, no shout, no self-interrupt. SOLUTION: AdaptiveDTD (core/engines/_dtd.py) -- fused self-calibrated z-scores (raw/residual/coherence) from per-device echo-only control charts, fire on weighted SUM D>K, NO fixed margin. DECISIVE FIX was the FIRING LOGIC: dtd_confirm_frames=1 (per-frame) + capture-loop LEAKY integrator -- a real talk-over scored D=90-130 but flickered, so the old 3-consecutive rule discarded it. Weights (raw 0.2, resid 1.0, coh 0.0): z_resid is the discriminator (user voice isn't in the reference -> AEC can't cancel it -> lands in residual). Also: fast_model=gemma3:4b (latency 8-12s -> 0.4s), persona answer-directly, endpoint_high_confidence_floor=0. ===> #1 NEXT (owner-requested 2026-06-08): (A) STREAM TTS for long answers -- a long story feels like it waits for the whole LLM answer; _stream_and_speak already emits per-sentence so investigate the sherpa-path first-token latency (gemma4:12b main tier) / confirm incremental emit->TTS. (B) SMARTER ENDPOINTING -- don't reply on the user's mid-thought pauses; switch endpoint_detector to 'prosody' (Smart Turn v3 on disk) + confidence floor. (C) SenseVoice agreement-guard for STT. NOTE machine-local config.local.json: input_gate.enabled=false (re-enable for always-on), asr_final_backend='' (sense_voice reverted -- hallucinated). === PRIOR: DONE 2026-06-07 (BARGE-IN COHERENCE-PRIMARY + GEMMA 4 ON LINUX; merged feat/barge-in-coherence-primary -> main @fe0617b, then v2 @377d10e, 1357 green). v2 LIVE-MEASURED on the open ALC285: coherence-ALONE self-interrupted (echo incoherent ~0.88 overlaps voice); FIX = coherence 'user' verdict must ALSO clear the post-AEC residual floor (orthogonal: AEC kills echo ENERGY not its incoherence). Measured 0 self-interrupts across 5 runs at full volume. ===> #1 NEXT: confirm a REAL talk-over STILL fires (HUMAN, can't be machine-tested) -- `python -m core --engine sherpa`, talk over a long reply, must cut <~1s; if missed lower barge_in_residual_margin_db (10.0). Also re-confirm on the AT2020 USB mic (was unplugged). Full handoff: docs/session_2026-06-07_barge_in_coherence_primary.md. FULL HANDOFF: docs/session_2026-06-07_barge_in_coherence_primary.md (read first). HEADLINE: open-speaker barge-in is NOT a hardware limit -- it was an architecture bug. The live trigger was a LOUDNESS gate on the post-AEC residual (no clean operating point on a nonlinear speaker); coherence (the volume-independent user-discriminator) was OFF and, when on, fed the post-AEC residual (AEC removes the correlation it keys on -> misread cancelled echo as 'user' = the self-interrupt). FIX: coherence fed the RAW pre-AEC mic is now the PRIMARY trigger (True=fire/False=reject/None=fall through); level gates demoted to a None-fallback. Adversarial review found+fixed a control-chart STARVATION blocker (baseline froze at provisional 0.5 on persistently-over-threshold nonlinear echo -> warm-up seeding learns the true echo floor). Compute-stop leak fixed (cancel now closes the Ollama stream so the server stops generating at the barge point, not at GC). Calibration params (warmup_frames/sigma_k/var_alpha/baseline_alpha/provisional_baseline) plumbed to SherpaConfig. Gemma 4: Ollama 0.30.6, gemma4:12b both tiers in config.local.json device_profiles, model_probe 4/4+vision. ===> #1 NEXT (needs the MIC): LIVE-VALIDATE barge-in on the open ALC285 -- the raw-mic-coherence premise on the nonlinear speaker is theoretically sound + unit-validated but UNVERIFIED on hardware. Run `python -m tools.echo_probe` (echo-only -> self_interruptions=0, read learned baseline/margin), `python -m tools.interrupt_suite`, then `python -m core --engine sherpa` (talk over a long reply: MUST cut, MUST NOT self-interrupt). Tune w/o code: coherence_warmup_frames^, coherence_provisional_baseline^, coherence_confirm_frames^, lower barge_in_min_speech_sec (0.8) for a snappier cut. ref_delay stays 0 until a fresh echo_probe ERLE sweep says otherwise. ALSO: core/runlog.py keeps 20 run bundles but ~50 are committed -> every run dirties git (bump SPEAKER_KEEP_RUNS or trim). === PRIOR: DONE 2026-06-05 pt3 (IMAGE-CONTEXT FEED; merged feat/image-context-feed -> main @d53a2db). core/capabilities.py forwards images (context['images'] per-turn OR ambient image_provider()) -> stream(images=), forces main/multimodal tier (fast 1b can't see images), floats sensitivity PRIVATE (§9.7). VoiceRuntime.set_current_frame(image)/clear_current_frame() let a HOST machine feed the current frame ambiently. Tests in tests/test_core_multimodal.py. REMAINING (user's future): the frame SOURCE (screen-grabber/camera that calls set_current_frame on a cadence). === DONE 2026-06-05 pt2 (TESTING UNIFICATION; merged chore/unify-testing -> main @e930b92, 7 commits, 1350 green). The suite is now ONE marker taxonomy -> 4 tiers: Tier0 unit/logic (no marker = default CI gate), Tier1 slow/e2e, Tier2 real_model (real weights over FIXTURES, no sound, self-skips w/o models), Tier3 live_output (REAL speakers, DOUBLE-GATED behind marker + SPEAKER_LIVE=1). New coverage: interrupt-race/failure-cascade/multimodal (Tier0), asr_live_quality (real_model), live_output smoke. Docs: docs/testing.md rewritten. Fix: thread image source -> turn context['images'] -> stream(images=). (2) optional pytest.ini tidy (smoke/dev/audio/backend still declared-unused). === EARLIER 2026-06-05 pt1 (VOICE DEMO, separate handoff docs/session_2026-06-05_voice_demo_live_validation.md): barge-in + smart-routing live demo via tools.live_session --inject; 2 landings @1335. FIXED in always_on_agent/react.py (dropped 'step by step' marker + FINAL falls back to own knowledge). Pulled gemma3:1b. MEASURED (inject, 4b): first-audio ~2.4-2.6s (endpoint ~1.1s + LLM ~1.2-1.4s + TTS ~50ms); routing splits fast/main correctly; barge 2/2 on 3 scenarios; add-on merge works. (4) short-barge (~0.4s 'Stop.') still rejected (barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.4). === PRIOR 2026-06-03 (voice pipeline STT/barge, for context) ===: DONE 2026-06-03 (voice-pipeline live session; 5 landings on main, all green @1322). FULL HANDOFF: docs/session_2026-06-03_voice_pipeline_stt_barge.md (read it first). HEADLINE: STT was garbled because the SenseVoice offline 2nd-pass was SILENTLY OFF (model never downloaded) -> every final was raw streaming-zipformer ('Are you there'->'Ario der'). FIXED by `python -m tools.setup_models --sense-voice` (machine-local model, gitignored -- EACH MACHINE must download it); the loader now WARNs if the backend is set but the model is missing. Also landed: addressing prompt reframe (answers questions again), DTLN onnxruntime thread-bound (aec_num_threads=1 -- fixed CPU 90-100% pegging -> deaf mic + frozen turns), CONFIRM-gate ('yes' answered instead of dead-ending), real_usage EMPTY/inventory. STILL OPEN (continue here, priority order): (1) BARGE-IN talk-over still flaky on the open nonlinear laptop speaker -- DTLN cancels echo for ASR but SUPPRESSES the user's voice during double-talk so the residual-floor gate rejects quiet barges. The real lever: aec_ref_delay_ms is pinned 19ms but the REAL acoustic delay is ~260ms (coherence delay= logs 258-337ms) -- CALIBRATE with tools/echo_probe.py + gate DTLN to run only while self._speaking (sherpa.py ~1161 engage gate has no speaking guard). Or trigger barge on the coherence detector (sees the user) not the DTLN-suppressed residual. (2) ENDPOINTING cut the user off mid-thought -- stopgapped to endpoint_min_silence_sec=1.1/high_confidence_floor=0; proper fix = endpoint_detector='prosody' (Smart Turn model ALREADY on disk at pretrained_models/sherpa/smart_turn/). (3) fast-tier hallucination; (4) a 'stop' KWS fast-path (kws not built). GOTCHAS: the user's C: disk was FULL (0 bytes) -- it CORRUPTED config.local.json to 0 bytes mid-session (I reconstructed it; it's gitignored + machine-local, see handoff for the working tuning values); free disk before live runs. faster-whisper installed in venv for tools/transcribe_run.py ground-truth (cache deleted to free space, re-downloads on use). === PRIOR SESSION (2026-06-03 barge-stop, for context) ===: (A) d53cac7 fix(barge): RC-2 -- stop_speaking() now AUTHORITATIVELY clears _speaking itself instead of leaving it to the playback worker's epilogue; if native tts.generate() wedges the worker never clears _speaking and the assistant goes DEAF for the rest of the session (capture loop keeps continue-ing past ASR; observed speaking=True ~15s after a 2s reply -> 'playback thread did not exit within 1.0s'). Cut path now clears _speaking + re-arms the one-per-run barge latch + resets echo/far-ref/AEC (all idempotent). Regression test = test_stop_speaking_clears_speaking_and_relatches_on_the_cut (wedged-worker case). RC-5 (observability only, no gate change): when VAD hears SUSTAINED speech during playback but the barge gate keeps rejecting it (and no barge fired this run), log once/episode + emit barge_in_rejected metric so 'user talked over but nothing fired' shows in the run bundle. (B) tools/transcribe_run.py -- timestamped ground-truth transcript (faster-whisper int8 CPU) of a run-bundle WAV to line up against the run log's ASR/barge events. (C) chore(logs) 57354e4 -- pruned stale 2026-06-02 bundles, added 2026-06-03 (incl. run-20260603-101952, a silent-mic capture failure kept as a record). NEXT: RC-5 has NO dedicated test (rejection branch is deep in _capture_loop, no light harness) -- add a capture-loop/replay test if it matters. P1 live-hardware story UNCHANGED from 2026-06-02: open-speaker barge-in is a HARDWARE LIMIT (post-AEC residual echo overlaps a normal voice, no clean operating point); reliable path = HEADPHONES. RC-2 removes the DEAFNESS failure mode that was confounding live tests -- re-run `python -m core --engine sherpa`, talk over a long answer, use the new barge_in_rejected metric to tell 'gate rejected me' from 'engine went deaf'. run-20260603-101952 was a SILENT MIC (avg_rms=0.0) = the known intermittent AT2020 USB-mic problem, not a code bug; re-seat/re-select (python -m sounddevice) before the next live session. Prior 2026-06-02 FIFO/AEC context: see docs/session_2026-06-02_playback_fifo_aec_alignment.md. (1) 554b300 -- recovered the PC-interrupted playback rewrite: TTS flows through a callback-driven OutputStream + bounded PlaybackFIFO so the AEC far-end reference is teed from the audio callback at the TRUE playback position (far->near lag now inside DTLN's +/-60ms); barge-in/shutdown flush the FIFO. Adversarial-reviewed (6 minor, 0 blockers); abort/flush contract documented (a true rollback is unsound under concurrent flush/drain), direct _audio_cb tests, pinned notify wake-path. (2) 088e055 -- the interrupt is now 'a bit slower, higher confidence': EchoCoherenceDetector requires coherence_confirm_frames (default 2) CONSECUTIVE over-threshold frames before firing, so a one-off nonlinear-echo spike can't self-interrupt; identity-free (speaker-ID untouched, stays a separate feature). NEXT (P1, needs the mic): AEC on-hardware -- enable aec in config.local.json, calibrate aec_ref_delay_ms + measure post-AEC ERLE with tools/echo_probe.py now that the far-end ref is aligned; live-tune coherence_confirm_frames (2 vs 3) on a real talk-over; then Smart Turn v3 live floor A/B. Latent real-time item in backlog: move coherence note_playback off the audio callback (lock-free SPSC) before raising coherence_ring_ms. (3) AEC CALIBRATION (tools b5ee64a): the FIFO arch fixed the DTLN far-ref sync -- tools.echo_probe ERLE sweep shows cancellation PEAKS at aec_ref_delay_ms=0 (~10-16 dB live) vs 0.8 dB at the old 94ms; built tools/interrupt_suite.py (mic x strategy sweep) + ERLE/override flags in echo_probe. OPEN-SPEAKER BARGE-IN NOW WORKS (self_interruptions=0): config.local.json set to aec dtln ref_delay=0, identity gate OFF (speaker_gate_input=false, interrupt is identity-free), coherence OFF (conflicts with AEC), aec_relaxed_margin_db=6, barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.6. (4) FIRST live test MISSED the real barge -> root cause: _looks_like_user gated the residual vs playback_level (required user LOUDER than the speaker -- wrong post-AEC). FIXED (b33f525): post-AEC barge now gates on loudness_admits(residual vs _ambient_rms) -- echo-only residual ~0.02, real barge ~0.4 (~26dB above floor). Tuned to self_int=0 at input_loudness_margin_db=15 + barge_in_min_speech_sec=0.8. STILL UNVERIFIED (needs a human): that a REAL barge now fires -- run `python -m core --engine sherpa`, talk over a long answer (should cut off after ~0.8s). The residual math says it WILL fire (0.4 >> ambient+15dB ~0.11). If MISSED: lower min_speech->0.5 / loudness margin->12. (5) ITERATION 3 (e04bb8d): live test had GARBLED TTS (wrong speed/pitch) + still missed barge. FIXED playback rate: device rejects 22050 but PortAudio sometimes 'opened' it anyway -> ran at wrong rate; now probes a supported rate via check_output_settings up front and plays at 44100+resample (portable). FIXED floor-creep: _ambient_rms now FREEZES its rise on bursts (>+6dB) so a sustained talk-over can't raise its own bar (self-calibrating). HONEST CONCLUSION: open ALC285 barge-in is a HARDWARE LIMIT -- the post-AEC residual echo spikes (~0.1-0.3) overlap a normal voice (~0.2-0.4) and vary run-to-run, so a level/sustain gate has NO clean operating point (self_int measured non-monotonic across margins). Best-effort config left (margin 18, sustain 0.8). RELIABLE PATH = HEADPHONES (echo-free -> barge trivially works, snappy; min_speech~0.3, margin~6, aec off). Auto-calibration delivered: playback rate (per-device), AEC delay (FIFO -> ~0), noise floor (freeze-on-burst), coherence margin (EWMA) -- but can't beat the nonlinear-speaker echo. (6) SESSION-ANALYSIS FIXES (9dc9a55): voice fix + barge-fire confirmed live (barge_in_latency~0). Fixed 3 problems from run-20260602-235431: (a) AEC ran on EVERY block incl. no-playback -> DTLN distorted CLEAN speech -> ASR garble; now gated on far-end energy (RMS>1e-4). (b) SenseVoice 2nd-pass hallucinated tiny clips ('SO'->'I then.', 'WHAT'->'I.'); asr_final_min_sec 0.0->0.5 so sub-0.5s clips use the streaming final. (c) barge-in still used speaker-ID via speaker_gate_input; made it IDENTITY-FREE (removed identity from _looks_like_user; speaker-ID gates FINALS only now -- fully decoupled). Not code bugs: TTS queue depth 26 on a long answer = working-as-designed (LLM faster than TTS; barge/stop interrupts it; queue has drop-oldest backpressure); RAM/VRAM ~90% = the gemma3:12b+4b model sizes. 1282 passed. See docs/session_2026-06-02_playback_fifo_aec_alignment.md.
